};

fs.writeFileSync(tmpPath, JSON.stringify(awInfo, null, 2));
console.log('Generated aw_info.json at:', tmpPath);

core.setOutput('model', awInfo.model);

2026-02-27T17:25:00.7303957Z The documentation consistency review is complete. Here's a summary of all 3 inconsistencies found and fixed across the `documentation/` folder:
2026-02-27T17:25:00.7304749Z 
2026-02-27T17:25:00.7304823Z ---
2026-02-27T17:25:00.7304943Z 
2026-02-27T17:25:00.7454802Z ### Changes Made
2026-02-27T17:25:00.7455037Z 
2026-02-27T17:25:01.2784141Z #### 1. `Quickstart-on-how-to-write-tests.md` – Mocha/Chai → Vitest
2026-02-27T17:25:01.5547389Z The code examples used `mocha` and `chai` APIs that are incompatible with the current test framework (`vitest`):
2026-02-27T17:25:02.1105926Z - Replaced `import { assert } from "chai"` with vitest imports in both `glossary.spec.ts` and `monitor.spec.ts` examples
2026-02-27T17:25:02.3825758Z - Removed `import { Context } from "mocha"` — replaced with `TestInfo` from `@azure-tools/test-recorder`
2026-02-27T17:25:02.6544101Z - Updated `new Recorder(context.currentTest)` → `new Recorder(context)`
2026-02-27T17:25:02.9423338Z - Changed `beforeEach(async function (this: Context) {` and hook callbacks to arrow function syntax
2026-02-27T17:25:03.2067976Z - Updated the recorder version reference from `3.x.y` → `4.x.y` with a note about Vitest
2026-02-27T17:25:03.2068459Z 
2026-02-27T17:25:03.4824998Z #### 2. `using-azure-identity.md` – Incomplete `DefaultAzureCredential` chain
2026-02-27T17:25:04.5704403Z The docs listed only `EnvironmentCredential` and `ManagedIdentityCredential` in the `DefaultAzureCredential` chain, but the actual implementation (verified in `sdk/identity/identity/src/credentials/defaultAzureCredential.ts`) tries 7 types. Added the missing: `WorkloadIdentityCredential`, `VisualStudioCodeCredential`, `AzureCliCredential`, `AzurePowerShellCredential`, and `AzureDeveloperCliCredential`.
2026-02-27T17:25:04.5706076Z 
2026-02-27T17:25:04.8104011Z #### 3. `resolve-pnpm-lock-merge-conflict.md` – Missing `/` in shell command
2026-02-27T17:25:05.1745319Z The combined `git add` command was missing a `/` path separator before `pnpm-lock.yaml`, causing it to fail with "pathspec did not match any files" on any directory outside the repo root.
2026-02-27T17:25:05.1746015Z

2026-02-27T17:25:20.0719348Z {"timestamp":"2026-02-27T17:24:07.0795897Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"safeoutputs","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"body":"## Summary\n\nThis PR fixes three categories of documentation inconsistencies found across the `documentation/` folder.\n\n---\n\n### 1. `Quickstart-on-how-to-write-tests.md` – Update code examples from Mocha/Chai to Vitest\n\n**Problem:** The code examples in the test quickstart guide used `mocha` and `chai` imports and patterns (`import { assert } from \"chai\"`, `import { Context } from \"mocha\"`, `this.currentTest`, `function()` syntax in hooks), but the repository switched to **Vitest** as the test framework (in `@azure-tools/test-recorder` v4). The existing examples would not work as written.\n\n**Fix:**\n- Replace `import { assert } from \"chai\"` with `import { describe, it, assert, beforeEach, afterEach } from \"vitest\"` in both example test files.\n- Remove `import { Context } from \"mocha\"` and replace the `Context` type with `TestInfo` from `@azure-tools/test-recorder`.\n- Update `createRecorder(context: Context)` → `createRecorder(context: TestInfo)`.\n- Change `new Recorder(context.currentTest)` → `new Recorder(context)`.\n- Convert `beforeEach(async function (this: Context) {` and `afterEach(async function () {` to arrow function forms (`(ctx) =\u003e {` and `() =\u003e {`).\n- Update `createRecorder(this)` → `createRecorder(ctx)`.\n- Update the recorder version reference from `3.x.y` to `4.x.y` and note Vitest as the test framework.\n- Add `.js` extensions to relative imports per ESM conventions used in the repo.\n\n**Verified against:** `sdk/purview/purview-datamap-rest/test/public/`, `sdk/monitor/arm-monitor/test/monitor.spec.ts`, `sdk/test-utils/recorder/README.md`, and `sdk/test-utils/recorder/src/testInfo.ts`.\n\n---\n\n### 2. `using-azure-identity.md` – Fix incomplete `DefaultAzureCredential` credential chain\n\n**Problem:** The documentation listed only `EnvironmentCredential` and `ManagedIdentityCredential` as the credentials tried by `DefaultAzureCredential`, but the actual implementation (`sdk/identity/identity/src/credentials/defaultAzureCredential.ts`) tries 7 credential types.\n\n**Fix:** Added the missing credential types to the list:\n- `WorkloadIdentityCredential`\n- `VisualStudioCodeCredential`\n- `AzureCliCredential`\n- `AzurePowerShellCredential`\n- `AzureDeveloperCliCredential`\n\n**Verified against:** `sdk/identity/identity/src/credentials/defaultAzureCredential.ts` JSDoc.\n\n---\n\n### 3. `resolve-pnpm-lock-merge-conflict.md` – Fix missing path separator in `git add` command\n\n**Problem:** The combined shell command at the end of the file had a typo: the `git add` invocation was missing a `/` path separator before `pnpm-lock.yaml`:\n```shell\n# Before (broken)\ngit add `git rev-parse --show-toplevel`pnpm-lock.yaml\n\n# After (fixed)\ngit add `git rev-parse --show-toplevel`/pnpm-lock.yaml\n```\nWithout the `/`, the command would fail with a \"pathspec did not match any files\" error.\n\n**Fix:** Added the missing `/` separator.\n\n---\n\n## Testing\n\nDocumentation-only changes; no code was modified. All changes were verified against actual source code and test files in the repository.","branch":"docs/fix-documentation-inconsistencies","title":"Fix documentation inconsistencies in documentation/ folder"},"name":"create_pull_request"}}}
